title: Send an SR1 medical evidence form
body: "Overview   You can send medical evidence with an SR1 form to support a benefit claim made with the Special Rules.   In most cases, the Special Rules allow people nearing the end of life to:    get faster, easier access to certain benefits  get higher payments for certain benefits  avoid having a medical assessment   Use this service to send an SR1 form to the Department for Work and Pensions if you have a patient who:    has a progressive disease and  as a consequence of that disease, you would not be surprised if they were to live for less than 12 months    There is a different process for healthcare professionals to support patients in Northern Ireland.   Who can send an SR1 form   To send an SR1 form, you must be a:    consultant  general practitioner  hospice doctor  senior specialist nurse (such as a clinical nurse specialist, advanced nurse practitioner)  specialty doctor    There are no negative consequences for the clinician or patient if a patient who claims under the Special Rules lives longer than expected.   General practitioners and GMC-registered consultants can claim a fee for completing an SR1 form.   Send an SR1 form   You can send an SR1 form online, by email or by post.   What you will need   You will need to tell us the patient’s:    name, date of birth and address  diagnosis and other relevant conditions  date of diagnosis  awareness of their diagnosis and prognosis  date they are thought to have met the criteria for the Special Rules  clinical features that indicate a severe progressive condition  treatment plan    You do not need to give a specific prognosis. Use the same language you use when communicating with other clinicians.   You will also need to tell us your:    name  role  professional registration number  organisation’s phone number and address    Send online   Start now   Send by email   Email form.e-sr1@dwp.gov.uk to request an electronic version of the SR1 form and fee form.   Your email must be from a secure email address and include your:    surgery, hospital, or office address  phone number    Secure email addresses end in:    @nhs.net  @nhs.uk  @nhs.scot  @nhs.wales.uk  @gov.uk    Emails not sent from a secure email address will be automatically deleted.   You can save the SR1 form to your device. You do not need to sign either of the forms.   Email the completed SR1 forms to form.e-sr1@dwp.gov.uk.   Do not encrypt any part of the email.   Send by post   You can order paper SR1 forms using the DWP order form.   Post completed paper SR1 forms and fee forms to:    Personal Independence Payment (10) Mail Handling Site A Wolverhampton WV98 1AE    You must:    sign the completed form  post the original copy  use a separate envelope for each patient’s form    Alternatively, you can give the completed SR1 form to the patient or the person who requested it on their behalf. You must still post the fee form to us.   We cannot accept a printed copy of the electronic SR1 form.   Do not staple any documents together.   Get help   Email: online.sr1@dwp.gov.uk   We aim to respond within 3 working days.  "
